Ibn Haldun University Signs MoU with University of Sarajevo and International University of Sarajevo

04.04.2022

Ibn Haldun University (IHU) signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the University of Sarajevo (UNSA) and the International University of Sarajevo (IUS) in two separate signing ceremonies.

Ibn Haldun University (IHU) signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the University of Sarajevo (UNSA) and the International University of Sarajevo (IUS) in two separate signing ceremonies. On the 31st of March 2022, Prof. Dr. Atilla Arkan accompanied by the team of delegates visited UNSA where the signing ceremony took place. Furthermore, on the 1st of April 2022, our president and the team of delegates spent the morning at IUS, where the agreement was signed.

The establishment of the University of Sarajevo can be traced back to 1537, having been formally established in 1949. UNSA is comprised of twenty-five Faculties, three Academies and five research Institutes.

The International University of Sarajevo was established by the Foundation for the Development of Education in 2004–2005, comprising of 5 faculties.

The agreement on cooperation focuses on the exchange of academicians, researchers, and students, the organization of joint workshops, the establishment of joint study programs and other forms of cooperation.
